twitter_thread: 80% of programmers write code for AI assistants like Claude, but only 20% document for human colleagues.
Claude demands 300% more documentation than the average codebase, forcing clarity and precision.
Dox and Sphinx can cut documentation time by 40%, but 90% of teams use them inconsistently.
One inconsistent doc style can add 2 weeks to a project's timeline, making it a major bottleneck.
Self-explanatory code reduces comments by 50%; 'customer_name' is better than 'data'.
Avoid 'result' as a variable name; it's used 10,000 times in the average codebase.
What's the worst documentation experience you've had with a teammate? #programmerlife #documentation
pack.linkedin: 80% of programmers prefer writing code for AI assistants like Claude, with its strict 300% more documentation requirements. Using tools like Dox or Sphinx can cut documentation time by 40%, but consistency is key - 90% of teams use them inconsistently. Writing self-explanatory code with clear variable names can reduce comments by 50%. What's your experience with code documentation - have you found a way to make it efficient for both humans and AI assistants?
tiktok_hooks: I just spent 5 hours documenting my code for an AI, but my human teammates still don't get it
The #1 thing I wish I knew about code documentation before I started my career
Why I'd rather write code for a robot than explain it to my colleagues
pack.reddit_headline: 80% of programmers prefer documenting for AI - are we losing the art of human collaboration in code?
